findings.title: ApprovalRecord fields in task 1.1 diverge from spec
findings.detail: Task 1.1 lists fields `actor`, `timestamp`, `decision` but the spec defines `status` ('pending'|'approved'|'rejected'), `requested_at`, `decided_at`, `decision_actor`, `event_ids`, `phase_from`, `phase_to`. The task field list must match the spec exactly to avoid rework. Replace task 1.1 fields with: record_id, record_kind, run_id, phase_from, phase_to, status, requested_at, decided_at, decision_actor, event_ids.

findings.title: ClarifyRecord fields in task 1.2 diverge from spec
findings.detail: Task 1.2 lists `actor`, `timestamp`, `status: 'pending'|'answered'`, `context?` but the spec defines `status: 'pending'|'resolved'`, `asked_at`, `answered_at`, `question_context` (not `context`), and `event_ids`. The status value 'answered' must be 'resolved'. Replace task 1.2 fields with: record_id, record_kind, run_id, phase, question, question_context?, answer?, status ('pending'|'resolved'), asked_at, answered_at?, event_ids.

findings.title: InteractionRecordStore interface in task 2.1 mismatches spec
findings.detail: Task 2.1 defines `write(record)` but spec defines `write(runId, record)` with runId as a separate parameter. Task 2.1 includes `exists()` which is not in the spec, and omits `delete(runId, recordId)` which IS in the spec. Fix: change write signature to `write(runId, record)`, remove `exists`, add `delete(runId, recordId)`.

findings.title: Tasks 3.3-3.4 cover record creation but not record update
findings.detail: The spec requires updating existing records when decisions/responses arrive (e.g., update ApprovalRecord status from 'pending' to 'approved' with decided_at and decision_actor; update ClarifyRecord with answer and answered_at). Tasks 3.3 and 3.4 only mention 'create'. Add explicit subtasks or clarify that these tasks also cover reading existing records and writing updates on decision/response transitions, including appending event_ids.

findings.title: Reject payload record_id not covered in task 5
findings.detail: The surface-event-contract spec defines that reject payload optionally includes `record_id` when a pending ApprovalRecord exists. Task 5 does not include a subtask for adding optional record_id to the reject payload or its JSON schema. Add a subtask for this.

findings.title: No task for CLI entry point wiring of InteractionRecordStore
findings.detail: The spec requirement 'CLI entry points inject InteractionRecordStore' requires specflow-run advance to construct a LocalFsInteractionRecordStore and pass it to the core runtime. Task 2.4 creates the factory function createLocalFsInteractionRecordStore(projectRoot) and task 3.1 adds the optional records field to AdvanceDeps, but no task explicitly covers updating src/bin/specflow-run.ts (the advance subcommand at line ~229) to instantiate the store via the factory and inject it into the advanceRun call. The existing CLI pattern (lines 229-230 in specflow-run.ts) constructs runStore and workflow but does not yet include records. Suggest adding a task between 3.1 and 3.2 (or as task 3.2) to wire createLocalFsInteractionRecordStore into the specflow-run advance subcommand, following the same pattern as createLocalFsRunArtifactStore. Without this task, the factory is created but never called, and records will always be undefined in production.
